key_insight: The paper develops an admissible minimizing-movement FEM framework for geometric gradient flows that separates normal motion from tangential mesh motion while preserving unconditional energy stability.
engineering_relevance: This gives a robust way to simulate curvature-driven surface evolution with better control of mesh quality, covering both mean curvature flow and surface diffusion flow.
method: Discrete time-step variational problem = normal metric dissipation + surface Dirichlet energy.
Use admissible weak constraints so the identity map remains feasible and tangential motion is independently selected.
Recover classical BGN and MDR schemes, and introduce admissible BGN plus relaxed MDR variants.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports existence and uniqueness under nondegeneracy assumptions, unconditional energy stability, and numerical comparisons of admissible versus classical schemes.
watch_item: Practical performance depends on the nondegeneracy of the evolving mesh and on enforcing the weak tangential-motion constraints consistently.
paper_title: A minimizing-movement framework for geometric gradient flows with admissible tangential motion
